creator: marino@FreeBSD.org
text:
Submitting a port doesn't guarantee it gets into the tree.  Working / building ports are removed all the time too, so every port is constantly evaluated for worthiness.

There could be 100 new committers, but if you submit a port without any kind of introduction of why it is a worthy addition, people aren't going to know what it is and assume that the submitter doesn't care enough to spend 5 minutes explaining what it is (regardless of the considerable amount of time the submitter probably spent developing the port).

Anyway, it was a tip.  You don't need to take my advice and apparently you didn't like it.  Good luck.  The PR CC'd me automatically, I'm removing that setting now.
